text_excerpt: News – Floella Benjamin Home About Me Biography Credits Appointments Literary Information Charity News News Archive Political Activity Floella Benjamin Productions Touching Success Gallery My Podcast Contact Open Menu BAFTA Fellowship acceptance speech at the BAFTA Television Awards in May 2024 More Expanding Horizons: My Continued Journey with Champions Speakers Working with Champions Speakers over the past five years has been nothing short of extraordinary. As someone who has spent a lifetime championing diversity, inclusion, and the power of resilience, I’ve always sought platforms that understand the importance of purpose-driven storytelling. Champions is one such platform — a leading UK keynote speakers agency that not only amplifies voices but aligns with my mission to make lasting change. More BBC Radio 4 Today programme More Front cover of Mayfair Times More Coming to England – The Musical More BAFTA Fellowship I am absolutely thrilled and delighted to have received the BAFTA Fellowship Award and feel totally blessed. Just wish my mum and dad were alive to celebrate this part of our family’s Windrush journey. More BIRMINGHAM REP HUB NAMED AFTER ME I am proud to have the Young Rep Hub named after me and to be the Patron of Youth and Education at Birmingham Rep, which is a wonderful privilege. More BAFTA Fellowship I am absolutely overjoyed that I am being awarded the BAFTA Fellowship at the BAFTA Television Awards on the 12th May 2024. More HARPER’S BAZAAR WOMEN OF THE YEAR I was super thrilled to be named as a Woman of the Year by Harper’s Bazaar in 2020 More EUROPEAN DIVERSITY AWARDS I was honoured and overjoyed to receive the European Diversity Lifetime Award in 2023 for my contribution to ‘Diversity and Inclusion’ for the last 50 years. More MULTI-CULTURAL BUSINESS & COMMUNITY AWARDS I was declared as the 'Motivational Queen' in 2022 and awarded the Multi-Cultural Business & Community Lifetime Achievement Award in Birmingham More WOMEN IN FILM & TELEVISION LIFETIME AWARD I was honoured to receive the Women in Film & Television Lifetime Award for my contribution to the creative industry. I was presented tome my one of my Playschool Babies, Nikki Amuka-Bird. I played her mum in the BBC drama ‘Line of Beauty’ and she told me I inspired her when he was a little girl so it was very appropriate. More TOP 50 WOMEN INFLUENCE LIST I was thrilled to be included in the Independent Women 2024 - Top 50 Influence List, for my contribution to many different issues in politics in the House of Lords. These included speaking out about the delays in the Windrush Compensation Scheme and demanding legislation to prevent children from easily accessing online pornography. More My autobiography: What are you doing here? I wrote my autobiography because so many people kept asking me how I got to where I am today. So at age 72 I decided to write it. What I didn’t realise was that I have done so much and could only include a fraction of what I have achieved in my incredible life…I kept thinking how blessed I am and saying to myself... ‘Who would have thought!' More CORONATION OF KING CHARLES III I was part of an historic event which was seen by millions of people across the world. More Order of Merit Two days before the late Queen Elizabeth II died she appointed me to the Order of Merit which is the highest honour in the land and is bestowed by the Monarch to only 24 living people. More National Windrush Monument In 2018 I was appointed by the then Prime Minister to Chair of the Windrush Commemoration Committee to be in charge of overseeing the creation of the National Windrush Monument celebrating and acknowledging the contribution made by Caribbean people to Britain. More My latest book My latest 'Coming to England’ book, a version for 4 year olds, comes out on the 8th October and I’m very excited. More My Statue at Exeter University Here I am with my statue which was commissioned by Exeter University when I stepped down as Chancellor after
